title: FDTDD active finding marker (v2)
description: The .tdd/findings/active.json marker that tracks a single active finding through Red → Green → Refactor → Closed. v2 (FDTDD Stage 1) extends the v2.1 PR #26 v1 schema with phase tracking, scoped unlock (test_files + prod_files), red-proof mechanical capture, and amendment ledger. Strict-mode compliant per v2.1.0 Bug 1 lesson: additionalProperties:false on every object AND properties == required.

schema_version.description: Marker schema version. v1 (=1) is the v2.1 PR #26 shape; v2 (=2) is the FDTDD Stage 1 shape locked in this slice. Backward-compat readers treat a v1 marker as v2 with phase=red, red_proof_accepted=false, empty test_files/prod_files.

finding_id.description: Stable identifier R<round>-F<finding>, e.g. R2-F3. Matches the v2.1 PR #26 format — slice 1 does NOT renumber existing findings.

started_at.description: RFC3339Z timestamp when finding-start.sh wrote the marker. Set once; never updated by later phases.

tier.description: Tier classification at start time. Drives whether Gate 1 / Gate 3 enforce. Determined by runner/lib/tier1.sh against the operator-declared prod_files (when present) or the finding description (fallback).

phase.description: Current FDTDD phase. red = failing test not yet accepted; green = Red proof accepted, prod fix in progress; refactor = test green, optional refactor window; closed = finding-finish ran. Drives Gate 1 (deny prod edits in red) and Gate 3 (lock test edits in green).

red_proof.description: Project-relative path to the red-proof markdown document. v1 field, kept for back-compat with existing finding-start.sh callers.

red_proof_hash.description: SHA-256 of red_proof file at marker-write time. Hooks verify the proof has not been mutated between accept-red and prod-edit. v1 field.

red_proof_accepted.description: True after finding-accept-red.sh (slice 2) has mechanically verified the declared failing test actually fails for the intended reason. False until then. Backward-compat: v1 markers (no field) read as false.

test_command.description: The literal command finding-accept-red.sh ran (e.g. `go test -run TestAuthRefreshRace ./internal/auth`). Verbatim, no shell-quote stripping.

exit_code.description: Non-zero exit code captured at accept-red time. Slice 2's finding-accept-red.sh REFUSES to transition phase to green if exit_code is 0 (test passed) — mechanical Red proof, not social. Range 1-255 because exit 0 means the bug is NOT reproduced.

captured_at.description: RFC3339Z timestamp when finding-accept-red.sh ran the test command.

output_tail.description: Last ~200 lines of test stderr+stdout, joined. Capped at 8000 chars. Lets future review verify the failure mode without re-running the test.

red_proof_record.description: BLOCKER 2 (Codex addendum 2026-06-08): proves Red is actually red. null until accept-red. Once set, transitions phase from red to green. finding-restart-red.sh (slice 4) clears this back to null.

items.description: Repo-root-relative, canonicalized (./x/../y → y), symlinks resolved via pwd -P. M1 path canonicalization.

test_files.description: Locked test files. Operator declares via finding-accept-red.sh. Gate 3 denies edits to these files during phase=green (strict_tdd) or asks (governed_tdd). MAY be empty for backward-compat v1 markers only — slice 2 finding-accept-red.sh refuses an empty list.

items.description: Repo-root-relative, canonicalized. Same rules as test_files.

prod_files.description: BLOCKER 1 (Codex addendum 2026-06-08): scoped unlock. Operator declares the prod files this finding can edit. Gate 1's allow-condition: red_proof_accepted=true AND file_path is in prod_files OR in the same Tier-1 directory tree as a file in prod_files. Empty list permitted only for backward-compat v1 markers; slice 2 finding-accept-red.sh refuses an empty list.

red_accepted_at.description: RFC3339Z timestamp when finding-accept-red.sh accepted Red proof. Null until then. Set once per Red→Green transition; finding-restart-red.sh (slice 4) clears it back to null.

green_started_at.description: RFC3339Z timestamp when phase transitioned to green. Same value as red_accepted_at today; separated for forward compatibility with Stage 2 (where the two events may decouple, e.g. mandatory review window between accept and green).

closed_at.description: RFC3339Z timestamp when finding-finish.sh ran. Null while phase != closed. After this, finding-finish.sh rotates the marker from .tdd/findings/active.json to .tdd/findings/closed/<finding_id>.json.

file.description: Repo-root-relative path of the test file amended.

rationale.description: Operator-declared reason for amending a locked test file during green phase (e.g. 'fix imports', 'helper placement', 'table case added without changing assertion'). Audit trail; not validated by gates.

amendments.description: MAJOR M3 (Codex addendum 2026-06-08): mechanical test-maintenance flow. Each entry records an operator-authorized test edit during green phase that does NOT regress to Red. Gate 3 allows edits to file F if the most recent amendment's file == F and ts is within configured window (slice 4 will define the window). Empty list by default; slice 4 wires the amend-red helper.
